Finding: Zero off-site web mentions; DDG Instant Answer API + brand search return no third-party coverage. Branded web mentions had 0.664 correlation with AI visibility in 75k-brand study.
Evidence / Query: 'OFAC sanctions screening API for AI agents'; 'agentmail OFAC'
Notes: #1 gap. No consensus = AI won't repeat the brand. Must earn 10-20 tier-1 third-party mentions before AI visibility moves.

Finding: Brand identity split: domain= sanctionsai.dev, product name in schema/title= agentmail, repo= kindrat86/agentmail, PyPI package= sanctions-mcp. Four names for one product.
Evidence / Query: Title tag, og:site_name, JSON-LD Organization.name all inconsistent
Notes: Pick ONE canonical brand name and use it identically in title, schema, domain, repo, PyPI. Models need consistent naming to consolidate entity.

Finding: Owns the new 'OFAC screening for AI agents / x402 payments' category via 182-URL pSEO site — but category has near-zero off-site definition. Competitors haven't entered yet.
Notes: First-mover on a real category (x402.org is live). Publish the category-defining original research/framework and label it with the brand name so LLMs attribute it.

Finding: No YouTube content. YouTube = ~5.6% of Google AI Overview citations; zero video presence means missing the AI-Mode / AIO citation pool.
Evidence / Query: 'how to screen wallet for OFAC' video
Notes: 2-3 search-hit videos: 'How to screen a crypto wallet for OFAC sanctions', 'What is Know-Your-Agent (KYA)'. Title=keyword, say keyword, timestamps in desc.

Finding: Not present on any 'best OFAC screening API' / 'sanctions screening tools' listicle. Reddit/HN/forum mentions = 0 detected.
Evidence / Query: title:best sanctions screening API; 'best OFAC API developer'
Notes: Top targets: dev-focused listicles (e.g. RapidAPI hub, PublicAPIs, any 'fintech compliance APIs' roundup); Reddit r/MachineLearning, r/ethfinance, r/CryptoCurrency; HN Show HN.

Finding: GitHub repo has 0 stars / 0 forks; PyPI has 1 release (v0.4.0). No social-proof signals for training-data presence.
Notes: Drive organic stars via Show HN, Reddit, dev Twitter. Star count feeds model consensus. PyPI: cut more releases so download/version history grows.

Finding: robots.txt 'Allow:' lists 7 paths that return 404: /compare, /ofac-sdn-list, /know-your-agent, /x402, /wallet-checker, /sanctions-screening-best-practices, /integrations. AI crawlers follow robots and hit 404s.
Notes: Either build those pages (they're real categories — should exist) or remove the Allow lines + 301 redirect to nearest live page. Broken paths advertised to bots erode crawl trust.

Finding: 1 broken URL in sitemap: /integrations/eliza → 404. Sitemap should never contain 404s.
Notes: Build the eliza integration page or remove from sitemap. ElizaOS / eliza is a real agent framework — page should exist.

Finding: Founder schema uses 'The Data Nerd' as Person name with LinkedIn /in/data-nerd-sanctions — looks like a persona, not a verified person. Risky for E-E-A-T signals.
Notes: Either verify the founder identity (real LinkedIn with activity) or drop the Person schema. Fabricated-looking entities can hurt trust.

Finding: No way to measure AI visibility today. No GA4 'AI traffic' channel, no 'How did you hear about us?' survey detected, no Brand Radar baseline.
Notes: (1) GA4 custom channel for AI referrers (chatgpt.com, perplexity.ai, you.com, etc.). (2) PostHog already live — add 'how did you hear about us?' with AI options on signup.
